files.keys_format: TripleKey[row_count] where TripleKey={uint32_t a, uint32_t b, uint32_t c}

storage_extensions.usage: mmap offsets.bin and keys.bin; use hash_triple(adsh, tag, version) >> 45 to route sub-filtered num tuples into morsel-local partitions, then exact-match against the tiny contiguous key slice for that partition instead of probing pre_adsh_tag_version_groups.hash.bin. Build one aligned uint32_t group-index permutation at startup so each matched partitioned key can jump to pre_adsh_tag_version_groups.offsets/rowids for payload lookup.

join_order.notes: Materialize a filtered dense lookup once from sub_fy_postings so the hot loop does one cache-resident adsh lookup that already encodes the fy=2023 predicate and returns name_code directly.

join_order.build: pre(adsh,tag,version partitioned exact directory + IS-only group payload)
join_order.notes: Use the existing 19-bit partitioned exact triple directory for cache-sized exact matching; keep an aligned partitioned group-index vector plus compact group->IS-plabel payload derived from pre_stmt_postings so matching avoids the 768 MB global hash and only touches stmt='IS' presentation rows.

scan.access_pattern: Sequential USD-postings scan with random-but-cache-resident sub lookup, then morsel-local partitioning of surviving tuples by pre triple hash so the pre join becomes an offsets lookup plus a tiny contiguous exact-key scan and IS-only payload walk.

data_structures.dim_filter: flat_array(filtered_sub_name_by_adsh) built from sub_fy_postings + sub.adsh + sub.name; kEmpty32 means fy!=2023 or missing filing
data_structures.fact_join: pre_built_index(pre.adsh_tag_version.partitioned_exact_p19 offsets+keys) + runtime uint32_t partitioned_group_index aligned to keys.bin + compact group_is_offsets/group_is_plabels derived from pre_stmt_postings and pre_adsh_tag_version_groups.offsets/rowids
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map(thread_local or morsel_local, key=(name_code, tag_code, plabel_code), value=(sum, count))

indexes.purpose: drive the scan from the USD slice instead of reading all num rows
enumerate fy=2023 sub rowids once and materialize the filtered dense adsh->name lookup used by every num probe
enumerate only stmt='IS' pre rowids and build compact per-group IS payloads so the hot join never scans non-IS presentation rows
provide stable group_index->rowid payload mapping for matched triples after the cache-friendly exact-partition lookup resolves the group
replace random probes into the 33554432-bucket global hash with a 4.1 MB partition directory and tiny contiguous exact-key slices

optimization_notes: Root cause: after the successful sub-first reorder, the hot path is still dominated by about 10.3M surviving USD facts probing `pre_adsh_tag_version_groups.hash.bin`, whose 33554432 open-addressed 24-byte buckets occupy about 768 MiB on disk / about 805 MB in memory, far beyond the 88 MB LLC, so the join is latency-bound on random cache misses. Strategy category B: replace that cache-exceeding probe pattern with cache-sized partitioned exact matching using the existing `pre.adsh_tag_version.partitioned_exact_p19` storage extension, where the 4.1 MB offsets directory is LLC-resident and each partition holds only about 16.5 triples on average (37 max). The revised plan also eliminates wasted work by materializing a filtered dense `sub` lookup for `fy=2023` and compact `stmt='IS'` group payloads from `pre_stmt_postings`, so the hot loop touches only qualifying sub rows and only IS presentation payloads while aggregating.
